title: 4) Diagnosis
text:
• First, check the position of the patient's neck and shoulders. They can give you a lot of
information, like the examples below.
①Neck is forwarded - Potential forward head and ﬂattened neck.
②C-curve of neck is not preserved - Flattened neck.
③One side of shoulder is elevated toward head - Tightened trapezius.
④One side of shoulder is elevated from the bed - Tightened pectoralis.
⑤Palpation of thickness and tightness of left and right SCM - Asymmetry.
⑥Palpation of thickness and tightness of  left and right trapezius  - Asymmetry.
~ !  ~
71
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
• These are possible common causes, but nothing decisive. Usually, causes are complex
involving at least a couple of factors. Carefully perform visual and tactile inspection of
the neck and shoulder. Also, you need to ask patients if they have pain or tension in
certain areas.
